title: Southwest Territory Created
description: Congress passes the Southwest Ordinance, establishing the Territory South of the River Ohio under federal governance.

title: Williamson Recommends Blount to Washington
description: Hugh Williamson writes to President Washington endorsing William Blount as the ideal candidate to govern the new territory.

title: Washington Nominates Blount as Governor
description: President Washington formally nominates William Blount to serve as Governor of the Southwest Territory.

title: Senate Confirms Blount
description: The United States Senate confirms William Blount's appointment as territorial governor.

title: "Where Ought the Governor to Reside?"
description: Washington writes to Secretary Knox asking the crucial question about where to establish the territorial capital.

title: Knox Recommends the Holston Settlements
description: Secretary of War Henry Knox advises Washington that the governor should reside among the Holston settlements.

title: Blount Arrives at Rocky Mount
description: Governor Blount arrives at William Cobb's home, establishing Rocky Mount as the seat of territorial government.

title: "Glass Windows" Letter Written
description: Blount writes his first letter from Rocky Mount, describing the house with its notable glass windows - a status symbol on the frontier.

title: Blount Reports on Cherokee Relations
description: Governor Blount writes to Secretary Knox about his initial meetings with Cherokee leaders and the state of frontier affairs.

title: Frontier Security Report
description: Blount details the military situation along the frontier and requests additional resources for territorial defense.

title: Treaty Preparations Begin
description: Blount outlines plans for negotiating a comprehensive treaty with the Cherokee Nation.

title: Treaty Conference Planned
description: Blount reports progress on arranging a major treaty conference at White's Fort.

title: Knox Sends Treaty Instructions
description: Secretary Knox provides detailed instructions for the upcoming treaty negotiations with the Cherokee Nation.

title: Final Treaty Preparations
description: Blount confirms that Cherokee leaders are assembling and the treaty conference will proceed as planned.

title: Treaty of Holston Signed
description: Forty-two Cherokee chiefs sign the Treaty of Holston at White's Fort, establishing peace and boundaries.

title: Treaty Results Reported
description: Blount sends a detailed account of the treaty negotiations and the terms agreed upon with Cherokee leaders.

title: Jefferson Acknowledges Treaty
description: Secretary of State Thomas Jefferson writes to Blount regarding the federal government's receipt of the treaty terms.

title: Post-Treaty Report
description: Blount provides updates on implementing the treaty terms and maintaining relations with Cherokee leaders.

title: Knoxville Gazette First Issue
description: George Roulstone publishes the first issue of the Knoxville Gazette, Tennessee's first newspaper.

title: Washington Proclaims Treaty as Law
description: President Washington issues a proclamation making the Treaty of Holston binding federal law, countersigned by Jefferson.

title: Gazette Reports Territory News
description: The second issue of the Knoxville Gazette covers territorial developments and frontier conditions. (Note: Biweekly schedule suggests Nov 19; Nov 12 date requires archival verification)

title: Capital Transition Update
description: Blount reports on preparations for relocating territorial operations to the new capital at Knoxville.

title: Gazette Covers Treaty News
description: The Knoxville Gazette publishes news about the ratified Treaty of Holston and its implications.

title: Cherokee Delegation Arrives in Philadelphia
description: Five Cherokee chiefs arrive in Philadelphia by ship to protest Treaty of Holston terms directly to President Washington.

title: Cherokee Chiefs Meet President Washington
description: Bloody Fellow leads Cherokee delegation in meeting with President Washington, who directs them to Secretary of War Henry Knox.

title: Cherokee Delegation Presents Demands
description: Led by Bloody Fellow, Cherokee chiefs present formal objections to Treaty of Holston, demanding increased annuity and settler removal.

title: New Year Gazette Edition
description: The Gazette reports on territorial affairs as the government begins its second full year of operation.

title: Capital Moves to Knoxville
description: The territorial government officially relocates from Rocky Mount to Knoxville, ending the Rocky Mount capital period.

title: Additional Treaty Article Signed
description: An additional article to the Treaty of Holston increases the annual payment to the Cherokee Nation to $1,500. Cherokee delegation's successful negotiation.

title: Cherokee Delegation Departs Philadelphia
description: Cherokee chiefs depart Philadelphia with increased annuity secured, demonstrating Cherokee diplomatic power.

title: Gazette From New Capital
description: The Knoxville Gazette publishes from the new territorial capital, covering government transitions.

title: Spring Gazette Edition
description: The Gazette reports on agricultural developments and frontier news as spring arrives.

title: Treaty Anniversary Coverage
description: The Gazette marks one year since the Treaty of Holston signing with territorial updates.

title: Fall Gazette Edition
description: The newspaper covers harvest season developments and frontier military matters.

title: Little Turkey Warns of Lower Towns War
description: Cherokee principal chief Little Turkey sends urgent message distinguishing peace faction from war faction, warns of Spanish influence among Lower Towns.

title: Gazette Spring Report
description: The Knoxville Gazette provides updates on territorial governance and Cherokee relations.

title: Mid-Year Territory News
description: The Gazette reports on the growing population and development of the Southwest Territory.

title: Gazette Summer Edition
description: Coverage of territorial development as the region continues to grow and stabilize.

title: Gazette Winter Report
description: The newspaper covers ongoing territorial affairs and preparations for potential statehood.

title: Statehood Momentum Builds
description: The Gazette reports on the growing movement toward Tennessee statehood.

title: Tennessee Becomes 16th State
description: Tennessee is admitted to the Union as the sixteenth state, completing the journey from territory to statehood.

title: Gazette Celebrates Statehood
description: The Knoxville Gazette reports on Tennessee's admission to the Union and the new state government.

title: Bradley Map Published
description: Abraham Bradley publishes an important early map of Tennessee, documenting the new state's geography.

title: John Watts Demands Treaty Enforcement
description: Cherokee leader John Watts addresses President Washington, demanding formal boundary survey with Cherokee oversight and accountability for treaty violations.
